```python
import pytest
import re
from typing import Any

def camel_to_snake(s: str) -> str:
    return re.sub(r'(?<=[a-z])(?=[A-Z])', '_', s).lower()

class TestCamelToSnake:
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("input_str, expected", [
        # Happy path: standard camelCase conversions
        ("camelCase", "camel_case"),
        ("thisIsATest", "this_is_a_test"),
        ("simpleTest", "simple_test"),
        ("aB", "a_b"),
        ("XMLHttpRequest", "xml_http_request"),  # Starts with uppercase
        ("APIKey", "api_key"),
        
        # Edge cases: already snake_case, all upper/lower, empty, single char, numbers/special chars
        ("snake_case", "snake_case"),  # Already snake_case
        ("ALLUPPER", "allupper"),  # All uppercase
        ("alllower", "alllower"),  # All lowercase
        ("", ""),  # Empty string
        ("a", "a"),  # Single lowercase
        ("A", "a"),  # Single uppercase
        ("123", "123"),  # Numbers only
        ("camel123Case", "camel123_case"),  # Numbers in middle
        ("test_123", "test_123"),  # Underscore already present
        ("aBcDeF", "a_bc_de_f"),  # Multiple transitions
        ("Test", "test"),  # Starts with uppercase, one word
        ("testCaseWithNumbers123", "test_case_with_numbers123"),  # Numbers at end
        ("special@Case", "special@_case"),  # Special characters
    ])
    def test_happy_path_and_edge_cases(self, input_str: str, expected: str):
        assert camel_to_snake(input_str) == expected

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("invalid_input", [
        123,  # Integer
        None,  # None
        [],  # List
        {},  # Dict
        12.34,  # Float
    ])
    def test_error_cases_non_string_inputs(self, invalid_input: Any):
        with pytest.raises((TypeError, AttributeError)):
            camel_to_snake(invalid_input)
```
